mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated and CPIAUCSL is still firm, equity discount rates stay restrictive. That combination should pressure S&P 500 prices lower over the next month as multiples compress before earnings can reaccelerate.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal's Rank IC stays above 0.03 with the wrong sign over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and softer future demand, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as analysts cut sales expectations and risk appetite falls.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of months where UNRATE rises year-over-year, or if the rolling 3-year sign accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in RSAFS after a weak prior month indicates improving consumption and revenue momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through better near-term sales expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if sales-upturn episodes fail to outperform baseline on 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for contemporaneous CPIAUCSL trends.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO while CPIAUCSL is flat implies improved real activity without new inflation pressure, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month via higher expected earnings and stable discount rates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if episodes of rising INDPRO with stable CPIAUCSL do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the conditional effect flips sign in multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling PAYEMS together with low UNRATE indicates labor-market resilience and household spending support, which should keep S&P 500 returns positive over the next week by reducing recession risk premia.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is flat or rising while UNRATE stays at or below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-strength condition is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power versus PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals housing demand rollover and weaker future construction and consumption, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through cyclical growth disappointment.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-deterioration episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the effect is confined to only one short subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in FEDFUNDS during a restrictive policy regime lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples expand.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines do not lead to higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDPC1 growth combined with rising UNRATE identifies recessionary demand contraction, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings revisions and risk appetite deteriorate together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below trend and UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if recession-style combinations do not underperform baseline on 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal remains negative only when UNRATE is omitted.
